description: A designed Department of Labor card in portrait/social format pairing a triumphant hero photo with an embedded news excerpt. The top is a photograph of President Donald Trump in a dark suit and red tie descending the stairs of the blue-and-white presidential aircraft (Air Force One) with his right fist raised in a defiant/victory gesture. Overlaid in serif type is the slogan 'Ensuring American Jobs Go To AMERICAN WORKERS!' ('AMERICAN WORKERS!' in large heavy caps). The lower half is a white rounded panel headed by the masthead 'The Washington Post,' with serif body text reporting that the Labor Department's enforcement initiative 'Project Firewall' is described by experts as the first federal attempt to proactively enforce the H-1B visa program since 1990, that the DOL would investigate employers to ensure foreign-worker pay/conditions meet legal standards, and that companies could face penalties including back wages, civil fines, or being blocked from the program. The design technique is patriotic strongman imagery plus a credible news quotation to validate an anti-foreign-labor/H-1B enforcement message. Target audience: American workers and the political base.
notable_text: Ensuring American Jobs Go To AMERICAN WORKERS! | The Washington Post | Labor Department's enforcement initiative, dubbed Project Firewall ... proactively investigate employers ... foreign worker pay and working conditions
